skill.name: embedding-strategies
skill.description: Plan, compare, and optimize embedding models, chunking methods, and retrieval evaluation for semantic search and RAG systems.
skill.summary: Choose embedding models and chunking strategies for semantic search, RAG, and vector retrieval workflows.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown code fences as Ruby shell backticks, but these are documentation delimiters and not executable shell commands. The hardcoded URLs are reference links to vendor documentation and benchmarks, while sample embedding code may contact model providers only if a user runs it with their own environment.

low_findings.title: Runnable Examples May Contact External Embedding Services
low_findings.description: The OpenAI embedding example calls client.embeddings.create, and local sentence-transformer examples can download named models when run. This is expected for an embedding strategy guide, but users should review provider terms and avoid sending sensitive text without approval.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain direct model or API calls that can use network access when executed. The behavior is clear, but it is presented as user-run example code rather than hidden execution.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The resources section links to OpenAI, Sentence Transformers, and the MTEB leaderboard. These are normal reference links and do not execute automatically, so the static network findings are low risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only as markdown reference links in a resources section. I found no evidence that the skill fetches these URLs automatically.

low_findings.title: False Positive Shell Backtick Detection
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported shell backtick execution at multiple locations, but the marked text is markdown code fencing around Python examples and diagrams. I found no shell command execution, no subprocess use, and no instruction to run hidden commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact locations are markdown fences surrounding non-shell content. Semantic review found documentation examples, not executable backtick interpolation or shell invocation.

content.value_statement: Teams often struggle to choose embedding models, chunk sizes, and evaluation methods for retrieval systems. This skill provides practical templates and decision guidance for semantic search and RAG applications.
actual_capabilities: Compares common embedding models by dimensions, token limits, and best-fit use cases.
Provides OpenAI and sentence-transformer embedding examples for single and batched text inputs.
Explains token, sentence, semantic section, and recursive chunking approaches.
Shows domain-specific and code-specific embedding pipeline patterns.
Defines retrieval evaluation metrics such as precision, recall, MRR, and NDCG.
Lists best practices for normalization, batching, caching, and model consistency.
limitations: Does not benchmark models on your private corpus without additional data and tooling.
Includes sample code that may need fixes, dependencies, and provider credentials before use.
Does not manage vector databases, indexing infrastructure, or production deployment.
Model lists and token limits may become outdated as providers release new versions.

use_cases.title: Design a RAG Retrieval Pipeline
use_cases.description: Select a model, chunking method, and evaluation plan for a knowledge base or documentation assistant.

use_cases.title: Improve Search Quality
use_cases.description: Compare retrieval metrics and adjust chunk size, overlap, preprocessing, and model choice.

use_cases.title: Plan Domain Embeddings
use_cases.description: Adapt embedding workflows for legal, code, multilingual, or specialized document collections.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Starter Model
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning a first semantic search feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose an embedding model for a semantic search app. My corpus is [describe documents], users ask [describe queries], and my budget is [budget].

prompt_templates.title: Design Chunking Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Recommend a chunking strategy for [document type]. Include chunk size, overlap, preprocessing, metadata, and risks for retrieval quality.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Retrieval Experiments
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an evaluation plan comparing [model A], [model B], and [model C]. Use precision, recall, MRR, and NDCG with a labeled query set.

prompt_templates.title: Architect a Domain Pipeline
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user designing a production embedding workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a domain-specific embedding pipeline for [domain]. Cover model selection, chunking, metadata, batching, caching, monitoring, and failure handling.

output_examples.input: A team needs embeddings for a support knowledge base with short articles and multilingual tickets.
output_examples.output: A model shortlist with multilingual support, a sentence-aware chunking plan, metadata fields, and retrieval metrics for validation.

output_examples.input: A code search project returns irrelevant functions for natural language queries.
output_examples.output: A code-focused embedding plan that preserves class and function context, evaluates query-document pairs, and avoids mixing model spaces.

output_examples.input: A RAG system has high cost and slow indexing for long documents.
output_examples.output: A batching and chunk-size review with options for smaller dimensions, caching, and targeted re-embedding of changed content.

best_practices: Evaluate embedding choices with labeled queries before committing to a model or dimension size.
Keep one embedding model per index unless you rebuild all vectors into the same vector space.
Preserve useful metadata during chunking so retrieval results can be filtered and explained.
anti_patterns: Do not send sensitive documents to external embedding APIs without data approval.
Do not choose chunk sizes only by token limits without checking semantic boundaries.
Do not optimize retrieval from anecdotal examples when a labeled evaluation set is available.

faq.question: Does this skill generate embeddings directly?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and sample patterns for embedding workflows that users can adapt and run separately.

faq.question: Can it help choose between hosted and local models?
faq.answer: Yes. It compares hosted and local options by accuracy, cost, latency, dimensions, and domain fit.

faq.question: Does it support RAG applications?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance focuses on semantic search and RAG retrieval quality, including chunking and evaluation.

faq.question: Does it include security guidance?
faq.answer: It does not provide a full security review, but it highlights practical risks such as external API use and sensitive text handling.

faq.question: Can it tune embeddings for code search?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a code-specific pipeline pattern that preserves surrounding context for better code retrieval.

faq.question: How current are the model recommendations?
faq.answer: They reflect the skill file content and should be checked against current provider documentation before production use.
